title: Nonmonetary Exchange — Substance Decides the Gain
event: Hollis Co. trades an old machine (cost $90,000, accumulated depreciation $60,000, fair value $36,000) plus $10,000 cash for a new machine. Prepare the exchange entry.

entries.label: FV basis — recognize the full 6,000 gain

lines.label: FV given 36,000 + cash 10,000
lines.why: With commercial substance, the new asset records at the fair value of what was surrendered.
lines.trap: FV of the OLD asset plus cash paid — not the old asset's book value.

lines.label: old contra cleared
lines.why: The old machine's accumulated depreciation dies with it.
lines.trap: Same disposal mechanics as a sale — this line always clears.

lines.label: original cost off
lines.why: The old asset exits at original cost.
lines.trap: Cost off, contra off — book value leaves in two pieces.

lines.label: boot paid
lines.trap: Cash PAID adds to the new asset's basis; cash RECEIVED would change the analysis.

lines.label: FV 36,000 − BV 30,000
lines.why: Commercial substance = economic positions genuinely changed = recognize the full gain now.
lines.trap: The gain measures FV against BOOK VALUE of the old asset — the exchange's own version of the disposal rule.

entries.label: BV basis — the 6,000 gain hides in the new asset

lines.label: BV 30,000 + cash 10,000
lines.why: No commercial substance (and cash paid): the gain defers by recording the new asset at book value plus cash.
lines.trap: The 6,000 unrecognized gain lowers the new basis — it surfaces later as smaller depreciation.

lines.label: old contra cleared
lines.trap: Disposal mechanics don't change — only the gain treatment does.

lines.label: boot paid
lines.trap: No gain line — the entry balances at the deferred basis, which is the whole point.

memorize.body: Commercial substance = future cash flows genuinely change → recognize gains. Without it, gains defer (losses are ALWAYS recognized, either way).
Losses never defer — conservatism cuts one direction. A trade at FV below BV books the loss under both variants.

questions.prompt: Old machine BV $30,000, FV $36,000, commercial substance exists. Gain recognized = ?

distractors.feedback: Deferral is the NO-substance treatment. With commercial substance, the full FV-over-BV gain is recognized: 6,000.
That's the old asset's fair value. The gain is FV minus BOOK VALUE: 36,000 − 30,000 = 6,000.

build.scaffold: Clear the old asset both ways. Substance → new at FV + cash, gain recognized. No substance → new at BV + cash, gain buried.
